content.text:
<user_query>
Create a modern, responsive website using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for a grill restaurant called “Lankkurullakioski”.

Style:

Dark modern grill/burger restaurant aesthetic
Black, dark gray, and warm orange color palette
Large hero section with background image
Rounded menu cards
Fully responsive for mobile devices
Smooth hover animations
Sticky navigation bar
Smooth scrolling

Website Structure:

Hero Section
Include:
Large heading: “Lankkurullakioski”
Subtitle: “The tastiest grill food in Tampere”
CTA button: “View Menu”
Opening Hours Section
Display:
Monday: 10:00 - 21:00
Tuesday: 10:00 - 21:00
Wednesday: 10:00 - 21:00
Thursday: 10:00 - 21:00
Friday: 10:00 - 00:00
Saturday: 10:00 - 00:00
Sunday: 10:00 - 21:00
Menu Section
Create categories:
Burgers
Extras
Grill Food / Snacks
Drinks / Kiosk Products

Each menu item should appear in a stylish card containing:

item name
description
highlighted price aligned to the right

Add these burgers:

Classic Burger — 10€
Beef patty, cheese, caramelized red onion, house mayonnaise, tomato, lettuce, pickles
Rye Burger — 12€
Beef patty, egg, processed cheese, house mayonnaise, tomato, lettuce, pickles
Double Burger — 14€
Double beef patties, caramelized red onion, Koskenlaskija cheese, tomato, lettuce, pickles
Chicken Pineapple Burger — 12€
Chicken, pineapple, cheese, caramelized red onion, house mayonnaise, tomato, lettuce, pickles
Mushroom Burger — 12€
Mushroom patty, house mayonnaise, tomato, lettuce, pickles
Lankkurulla Burger — 14€
Beef patty, egg, Koskenlaskija cheese, pineapple, caramelized red onion, house sauces, tomato, lettuce, pickles
Heimo Burger — 14€
Beef patty, egg, double cheese, devil’s jam, house sauces, cabbage, tomato, pickles

Extras:

+4€ Double patty
+4€ Fries with burger
+1.5€ Double mayonnaise
+1.5€ Bacon, egg, pineapple, extra cheese, Koskenlaskija cheese, fried onions, pickled red onion, crispy onion, or blue cheese

Add all remaining grill foods, snacks, drinks, and kiosk products in their own categories as well.

Location Section
Display:
Ahlmanintie 23B, 33100 Tampere

Embed a Google Map.

Footer
Include:
“Lankkurullakioski”
Tampere
Copyright text

Technical Requirements:

Generate all files:
index.html
style.css
script.js
Use Google Fonts
Add fade-in animations on scroll
Use Font Awesome icons
Make the website look like a premium burger restaurant website
</user_query>

Creating the restaurant site: checking the workspace and adding `index.html`, `style.css`, and `script.js`.

[REDACTED]

Creating the three site files in the project workspace with a premium dark grill aesthetic, full menu categories, and requested features.

[REDACTED]

Adding `scroll-margin-top` so in-page links don’t hide section titles under the sticky nav.

[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Here is what was added for **Lankkurullakioski** in your workspace:

### Files
- `C:\Users\Aira\.cursor\projects\empty-window\index.html` — structure, hero, hours, full menu (burgers, extras, grill/snacks, drinks/kiosk), location + map iframe, footer  
- `C:\Users\Aira\.cursor\projects\empty-window\style.css` — dark theme, orange accents, sticky blurred nav, rounded cards, hover motion, responsive grid, scroll-reveal styles, `prefers-reduced-motion` handling  
- `C:\Users\Aira\.cursor\projects\empty-window\script.js` — mobile nav, smooth `scrollIntoView`, Intersection Observer fade-ins, copyright year  

### Design & behaviour
- **Fonts:** [Outfit](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Outfit) (headings) + [DM Sans](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/DM+Sans) (body) via Google Fonts  
- **Icons:** Font Awesome 6 (CDN on cdnjs)  
- **Hero:** Full-width background (Unsplash burger/grill image), gradient overlay, **View Menu** CTA, subtle scroll cue  
- **Menu cards:** Name + description with **price top-right** in accent orange; featured badges on **Lankkurulla** and **Heimo**  
- **Grill / snacks / drinks / kiosk:** Extra items with plausible descriptions and € prices (you can swap numbers anytime)  
- **Map:** Google Maps search embed for `Ahlmanintie 23B, 33100 Tampere, Finland`. If Google changes embed behaviour, use **Maps → Share → Embed a map** and replace the iframe `src` with their snippet  

Open `index.html` in a browser (double-click or “Open with Live Server”). For the map, a normal `https://` origin is best; some browsers restrict `file://` iframes more strictly.

If you want this in its own folder, Finnish copy only, or real prices for the invented items, say how you’d like it adjusted.

[REDACTED]

content.text: [REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Here is what was implemented.

### HTML (`index.html`)
- Sticky category bar **after** the menu intro, with four links: `#menu-burgers`, `#menu-extras`, `#menu-grill-snacks`, `#menu-drinks-kiosk`.
- Each block is a **section** with `class="menu-block menu-category"` and the matching **`id`**.
- First tab: `is-active` + `aria-current="true"`.

### CSS (`style.css`)
- **`html`** already had `scroll-behavior: smooth` (kept).
- **`--menu-cats-sticky-h`**: default in `:root`; **overwritten in JS** from the real sticky bar height so `scroll-margin` stays aligned.
- **`.menu-cats`**: `position: sticky`, `top: var(--header-h)`, blur, border, shadow; tightens on hover.
- **`.menu-cats__track`**: horizontal scroll, thin orange scrollbar, `scroll-snap-type: x proximity`.
- **`.menu-cats__btn`**: pill shape, dark fill, hover lift + border; **`.is-active`**: orange border, glow, layered shadow.
- **`.menu-category`**: `scroll-margin-top: calc(var(--header-h) + var(--menu-cats-sticky-h) + 0.85rem)` so anchors clear **site header + category bar**.
- **`prefers-reduced-motion`**: transitions/transforms disabled on category buttons (no motion on tab state).

### JavaScript (`script.js`)
- **Scroll spy**: `requestAnimationFrame`–throttled scroll/resize; scan line = header height + category bar height + offset; picks the last category whose top is above that line; handles “above menu” (first tab) and “below menu” (last tab).
- **Click (capture on `.menu-cats`)**: sets active tab immediately and **scrolls the active tab into view** in the horizontal strip (`inline: "center"`).
- **`syncMenuCatsHeight()`**: sets `--menu-cats-sticky-h` on load, resize, and `document.fonts.ready`.
- **Smooth scroll**: global hash links use `behavior: "smooth"` unless reduced motion is on (same as before, extended).

If you want the category bar to stay fixed after leaving the menu section, that would need a different layout (e.g. moving the nav outside `#menu`); right now it only sticks **while the menu block is on screen**, which matches “while scrolling through the menu.”

[REDACTED]

badges
title

orange/gold pill style

Add spacing using gap, padding, and margins
Keep a premium modern burger restaurant style

Create a separate top section inside each menu card for badges
Place the price in a dedicated container aligned to the top-right
Prevent text wrapping into the price area
Add max-width for text content if needed
Ensure long menu item names do not break the layout
</user_query>

content.text:
Updating CSS: replacing the menu-card block with flexbox layout and new badge styles.

[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Here is what changed.

### Structure (`index.html`)
Every menu `article` now follows the same pattern:
- **`header.menu-card__top`** — flex row with **`menu-card__badges`** (empty `div` with `aria-hidden="true"` when there is no tag) and **`menu-card__price-wrap`** wrapping **`menu-card__price`**.
- **`div.menu-card__main`** — **`h4.menu-card__name`** and optional **`p.menu-card__desc`** (full width under the top row).

Featured items use real badges in the badges slot:
- **House favourite:** `menu-card__badge menu-card__badge--house`
- **Spicy:** `menu-card__badge menu-card__badge--spicy` (`.menu-card__badge--hot` is still styled the same in CSS for compatibility)

### Layout & styling (`style.css`)
- **`.menu-cards`:** `align-items: stretch` so every card in a row matches height.
- **`.menu-card`:** `display: flex; flex-direction: column; height: 100%; gap` so content stacks cleanly and cards fill the grid row.
- **`.menu-card__top`:** flex row, `justify-content: space-between`, gap — badges use **`flex: 1 1 0; min-width: 0`** so they stay in the left column; **`.menu-card__price-wrap`** is **`flex: 0 0 auto`** so the price column does not shrink and stays top-right.
- **`.menu-card__main`:** `flex: 1`, column flex, `min-width: 0` so long text wraps instead of forcing into the price column.
- **`.menu-card__name` / `.menu-card__desc`:** `max-width: 100%`, `overflow-wrap: break-word`, `hyphens: auto` on the title for long names.
- **Badges:** no `position: absolute`; pill look with **gold/orange gradient** for house and **red gradient** for spicy.
- **Compact cards:** slightly tighter gap; **`.menu-card__main`** uses `justify-content: center` when there is only a title.
- **`@media (max-width: 480px)`:** slightly smaller padding, gaps, badge text, and price size.

Absolute positioning on badges was removed, so tags and price stay in separate flex areas and cannot overlap.

[REDACTED]

Tässä mitä tein:

### Kieli & rakenne (`index.html`)
- `lang="fi"`, UTF-8, suomenkielinen **title** ja **meta description**.
- **Navigaatio:** Aukioloajat, Ruokalista, Sijainti, Yhteystiedot (uusi ankkuri `#contact`).
- **Hero:** grilli & kioski, slogan suomeksi, CTA **Katso ruokalista**, vieritys **Vieritä**.
- **Aukioloajat:** otsikko **Aukioloajat**, viikkopäivät suomeksi, samat kellonajat kuin ennen.
- **Ruokalista:** osastot ja tuotteet kuten annoit (hampurilaiset isoilla kirjaimilla, kuvaukset täsmälleen).
- **Täytetty ruisleipä:** kuvaus **(kahvi +1€)**.
- **Tuplamajoneesi:** nimi + kuvaus riviltä sulkeissa (talonmajoneesi, valkosipulimajoneesi, paholaisen hillo).
- **Pekoni…** -lisärivi: koko lista **h4**-nimellä, hinta **+1,5€** (ei erillistä kuvausta).
- **Kuukautisside:** nimi + hinta **5€/pkt**.
- **Sijainti:** otsikko **Sijainti**, osoite rivillä, kartta `hl=fi`, iframe-otsikko suomeksi.
- **Yhteystiedot:** uusi osio `#contact`, otsikko **Yhteystiedot**, osoite **Ahlmanintie 23B, 33100 Tampere**.
- **Footer:** Tampere, Suomi + **Kaikki oikeudet pidätetään.**

### Sticky-kategoriat
- Linkit: `#menu-hampurilaiset`, `#menu-lisukkeet`, `#menu-grill-trahteet`, `#menu-juomat-kiosk`.
- Välilehtien tekstit: **HAMPURILAISET**, **LISUKKEET**, **GRILLIEVÄÄT / TRAHTEERIT**, **JUOMAT / KIOSKITUOTTEET**.

### `script.js`
- `categoryIds` päivitetty vastaamaan uusia `id`-tunnisteita.
- Valikon auki/sulku **aria-label** suomeksi.

### `style.css`
- Ruudukko: `minmax(min(100%, 280px), 1fr)` jotta pitkät suomenkieliset rivit mahtuvat paremmin.
- **`.section--contact`** tumma tausta ja yhteystiedon typografia.
- **`.menu-cats__btn`**: hieman pienempi, skaalautuva `font-size` pitkille isoille kirjaimille.

Suomen **ä** ja **ö** tallennetaan suoraan UTF-8-merkkeinä tiedostoon (ei HTML-entiteettejä). Animaatiot, hoverit, korttien flex-asettelu ja scroll-spy säilyvät ennallaan.

content.text: [REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Here is what was implemented.

### `theme.js` (new)
- Tries these paths in order: `assets/hero.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.png`, `.webp`, then `hero.jpg` variants in the site root.
- If none load, it uses the existing Unsplash burger URL (with `crossOrigin="anonymous"` so the canvas can read pixels).
- **Canvas (≈80px wide):** averages colours for “ambient” surfaces, picks the **most saturated** in-range pixel as **accent** (falls back to a warm amber if the image is dull).
- **Dark vs light:** `relativeLuminance` of the average < 0.42 → `html.theme-dark`, otherwise `html.theme-light`.
- **Blue night scenes:** if the accent hue is ~200–300°, it is blended toward warm amber so the brand stays “grilli / neon”.
- Writes **CSS variables** on `:root`: backgrounds, text, borders, accent + alpha helpers, hero image URL, **hero overlay** (dark scrim vs light white scrim), header / mobile nav / sticky menu bar translucency, featured & heat tints, badge borders/shadows, button text colour vs accent luminance.
- **CORS:** local files on `http://localhost` work; `file://` may block canvas reads — use a small local server if the theme does not update.

### `style.css`
- Extended **`:root`** defaults toward your kiosk reference: midnight blue surfaces, **#ffb347** accent, cream text.
- Surfaces, borders, shadows, buttons, category tabs, hours/menu cards, map, badges, and gradients use **`var(--…)`** so the theme can replace them.
- **Hero:** `background-image: var(--hero-bg-image)`; overlay uses `--hero-overlay-top/mid/bottom`.
- **`html.theme-dark` / `html.theme-light`:** `color-scheme` for native controls.
- **Smooth theme change** (when motion is allowed): shared `--theme-transition` on layout chrome, hero, tabs, buttons, cards (menu cards keep transform timing + colour transition).

### `index.html`
- `<script src="theme.js" defer></script>` in `<head>` (before `script.js`).

### `assets/.gitkeep`
- Keeps `assets/` in git; **put your hero file here as `assets/hero.jpg`** (or another supported name from the list).

**Behaviour:** After load, the script repaints the whole UI from the hero image. Bright photos get a lighter scrim and light surfaces with dark type; dark photos keep a dark UI with warm highlights. If you want only your photo with no burger fallback, add `assets/hero.jpg` and the first successful load drives the palette end-to-end.

Here’s the situation in plain terms:

**I can’t store the picture you sent** inside your project from this chat—there’s no way to turn that attachment into a real file on your disk. Only you can do that.

### What to do (about 30 seconds)

1. **Save your kiosk photo** into the project folder as:
   - **`assets/hero.jpg`**  
   or, if you prefer another format, one of these (same folder `assets/`):
   - `hero.jpeg`, `hero.png`, `hero.webp`  
   (`theme.js` already tries those names in order.)

2. **Refresh the site** (hard refresh: Ctrl+F5). The hero and the dynamic colours will follow **your** image.

### What we changed in the project

- The **old Unsplash burger** default is **removed**. The site expects **`assets/hero.jpg`** first.
- If that file is still missing, you get a **dark gradient** only (no random stock photo).

So: drop your image in as `assets/hero.jpg` (or another supported name above), and it replaces the old one automatically.

Tracing the hero image flow and fixing path/loading issues.

[REDACTED]
Using a real `` for the hero (more reliable than CSS `background-image` + variables) and fixing URL resolution for `file://` and local servers. [REDACTED]

Identifying issues: (1) Canvas `getImageData` can throw (e.g. CORS); the catch called `applyFallbackTheme()`, replacing the photo with a gradient. (2) `crossOrigin = "anonymous"` can break `file://` loads. (3) CSS-only backgrounds are less reliable than an ``. Fixing all three: [REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
Implementing an ``-based hero and fixing the canvas error path that replaced your photo with a gradient. Fixing `crossOrigin` for local files. [REDACTED]

content.text: [REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Moving `syncHeroVisual` to the end of `applyThemeFromPalette` (after color variables) and adding a default `img` `src`.

[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed. ### Why the picture disappeared 1. **Canvas errors** – If reading the image for colours failed (common with `crossOrigin` or strict browsers), the script called `applyFallbackTheme()` and **replaced your photo with a plain gradient**. 2. **`crossOrigin = "anonymous"` on local files** – For `file://` or some local setups that can **block** the image from loading at all. 3. **Only a CSS `background-image`** – Easier to break than a normal ``. ### What we did - **Real ``** behind the overlay, full width, `object-fit: cover`, so the hero always uses a normal image when a file exists. - **Default `src="./assets/hero.jpg"`** so the browser starts loading your file immediately (with `onload` → `is-visible`). - **`crossOrigin` only for `http(s):` URLs**, not for `file:` / local paths. - **More paths tried**: `./assets/hero.jpg`, `assets/hero.jpg`, `hero.jpg`, etc. - **Correct `URL(..., window.location.href)`** so paths work from the folder where `index.html` lives. - **If colour sampling fails**, we still run **`applyThemeFromPalette(KIOSK_STATIC, url)`** so **your image stays**; only the palette falls back to the default. ### What you should do 1. Put the file here (same project as `index.html`): **`assets/hero.jpg`** (Name it exactly that, or try `hero.jpg` in the **root** next to `index.html`.) 2. Open the site via **“Open with Live Server”** (or any local `http://` server), not only double‑click `index.html`, if the image still fails—some browsers are strict with `file://`. Structure should look like: ```text empty-window/ index.html theme.js style.css assets/ hero.jpg ← your photo ``` [REDACTED]

I need to add social links, which means using local assets and possibly removing Font Awesome. **Finalizing social icon styles**

I'll assume the social icons will be monochrome to match the theme. Additionally, I'll incorporate opacity changes and possibly use `box-shadow`. When hovering, I want the icons to scale up, applying transformations and filters.

Since we need to remove online icon libraries like Font Awesome, we'll ensure to use local assets instead. I'll reference `assets/facebook.png` and `assets/instagram.png`, and it’s essential to ensure they have target="_blank"!
**Implementing changes with ApplyPatch**

I'm ready to apply modifications using ApplyPatch for both `index.html` and `style.css`. I'll add ARIA labels for accessibility, ensuring that alt attributes are empty and aria-labels are set on the link.

In `index.html`, I'll place the social icons in the `nav__menu`, aiming for them to be first for better visibility after Yhteystiedot. For alignment, I'll create a list item with the class `nav__social` and set the anchors accordingly. 

In `style.css`, I'll define hover effects, adjusting styles to ensure the social links align correctly and look good across devices. I'll implement the changes now!

Lisäsin verkkosivulle **Facebook- ja Instagram-linkit** paikallisiin kuvakkeisiin (ei ulkoisia ikonikirjastoja).

### Mitä muuttui
- `index.html`: navbarin oikealle puolelle lisättiin kaksi uutta linkkiä:
  - Facebook: `https://facebook.com/share/18YYrcgSvb/`
  - Instagram: `https://instagram.com/lankarullakioski?igsh=Mmk2MjAwbGs1a2V4`
  - Mukana `target="_blank"`, `rel="noopener noreferrer"` ja `aria-label`-attribuutit.
- `style.css`: lisättiin temaattiset tyylit sosiaalikuvakkeille:
  - pienet ja siistit ikonirullat (pillo/rounded)
  - hover: lievä suurennus + “glow” accent-värillä
  - mobiilissa ne näkyvät automaattisesti hamburger-valikossa (koska ne ovat samassa `nav__menu`-listassa)

### Käytetyt icon-polut
Varmista, että löydät tiedostot projektista:

**Addressing icon transitions and accessibility**

I see that our transitions for icons might still apply, but the CSS for reduced motion only overrides specific classes. It seems the social icons aren't included in that reduction, even though the requirement didn’t mention it. For accessibility's sake, I’ll consider adding “transitions none,” though it's not mandatory.
